record.field: Directus is an open data platform that wraps any SQL database in instant REST and GraphQL APIs plus a no-code admin Studio.

Directus is an open data platform that wraps any SQL database in instant REST and GraphQL APIs plus a no-code admin Studio.

It connects to a new or existing Postgres, MySQL, MariaDB, SQL Server, SQLite or OracleDB schema and mirrors it as a fully managed backend, without forcing a predefined data model. Developers get auto-generated APIs, granular access control and authentication, while non-technical teams manage content through a customizable web interface. It is a popular choice for headless CMS, internal tooling, admin panels and any project that needs a database-first backend.

Key features
  • Instant REST and GraphQL APIs generated from any SQL database schema
  • No-code Studio for content editing, dashboards and data management
  • Granular role-based permissions, authentication and SSO/OAuth support
  • Event-driven automation with flows, webhooks and custom extensions
  • Draft and publishing workflows, asset/file management and image transforms
  • Native MCP server to let AI agents read and write content
Best for

Developers and teams building headless CMS backends, internal apps, admin panels or API layers on top of relational data. Note that Directus uses the source-available MSCL license; self-hosting is free for organizations under the Innovation Grant thresholds, with a commercial license required above them.

services: database: image: postgis/postgis:13-master volumes: - ./directus_database:/var/lib/postgresql/data environment: POSTGRES_USER: directus POSTGRES_PASSWORD: ${DATABASE_PASSWORD} POSTGRES_DB: directus healthcheck: test: - CMD - pg_isready - --host=localhost - --username=directus interval: 10s timeout: 5s retries: 5 start_interval: 5s start_period: 30s container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}_database' restart: unless-stopped networks: - internal cache: image: redis:6 healthcheck: test: - CMD-SHELL - '[ $$(redis-cli ping) = ''PONG'' ]' interval: 10s timeout: 5s retries: 5 start_interval: 5s start_period: 30s container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}_cache' restart: unless-stopped networks: - internal directus: image: directus/directus:11.12.0 ports: - '{{ PORT_HTTP }}:8055' volumes: - ./directus_uploads:/directus/uploads - ./directus_extensions:/directus/extensions depends_on: database: condition: service_healthy cache: condition: service_healthy environment: SECRET: ${DIRECTUS_SECRET} DB_CLIENT: pg DB_HOST: database DB_PORT: '5432' DB_DATABASE: directus DB_USER: directus DB_PASSWORD: ${DATABASE_PASSWORD} CACHE_ENABLED: 'true' CACHE_AUTO_PURGE: 'true' CACHE_STORE: redis REDIS: redis://cache:6379 ADMIN_EMAIL: admin@example.com ADMIN_PASSWORD: d1r3ctu5 container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}' restart: unless-stopped networks: - internal - agz-proxy-network networks: internal: driver: bridge agz-proxy-network: external: true

#!/bin/bash set -euo pipefail cd "$SERVICE_PATH" # Dump consistente de PostgreSQL (servicio en marcha) → $BACKUP_DATA_DIR. # Si POSTGRES_USER/POSTGRES_DB vienen vacíos (la imagen usa 'postgres' por defecto), # caemos a 'postgres': con $POSTGRES_USER vacío pg_dump intentaría el rol "root" del SO # y fallaría con «role "root" does not exist». docker compose exec -T database sh -c 'pg_dump -U "${POSTGRES_USER:-postgres}" -d "${POSTGRES_DB:-${POSTGRES_USER:-postgres}}" --no-owner -Fc' \ > "$BACKUP_DATA_DIR/db.dump" echo "pg_dump OK ($(wc -c < "$BACKUP_DATA_DIR/db.dump") bytes)"

#!/bin/bash set -euo pipefail cd "$SERVICE_PATH" # Levanta solo la DB, espera readiness y restaura (mismo default a 'postgres') docker compose up -d database for i in $(seq 1 30); do docker compose exec -T database sh -c 'pg_isready -U "${POSTGRES_USER:-postgres}"' && break sleep 2 done docker compose exec -T database sh -c 'pg_restore -U "${POSTGRES_USER:-postgres}" -d "${POSTGRES_DB:-${POSTGRES_USER:-postgres}}" --clean --if-exists --no-owner' \ < "$RESTORE_DATA_DIR/db.dump" # Verificación: la DB no quedó vacía (sh -c con dobles comillas; $VAR del contenedor con \$) N=$(docker compose exec -T database sh -c "psql -U \${POSTGRES_USER:-postgres} -d \${POSTGRES_DB:-${POSTGRES_USER:-postgres}} -tAc \"SELECT count(*) FROM information_schema.tables WHERE table_schema='public'\"") echo "tablas restauradas: $N" test "$N" -gt 0
